title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups
description: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Learners cumulatively produce a storage map and restore-tested backup plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
